Section Title:  > Use of SMS Data/Information > FAST Act of 2015:
Content:
Readers should not draw conclusions about a carrier's overall safety condition simply based on the data displayed in this system. Unless a motor carrier has received an UNSATISFACTORY safety rating under part 385 of title 49, Code of Federal Regulations, or has otherwise been ordered to discontinue operations by the Federal Motor Carrier Safety Administration, it is authorized to operate on the Nation's roadways.
Section Title:  > Use of SMS Data/Information > FAST Act of 2015: > Safety Measurement System:
Content:
The data in the Safety Measurement System (SMS) is performance data used by the Agency and Enforcement Community. Asymbol, based on that data, indicates that FMCSA may prioritize a motor carrier for further monitoring.
Thesymbol is not intended to imply any federal safety rating of the carrier pursuant to 49 USC 31144. Readers should not draw conclusions about a carrier's overall safety condition simply based on the data displayed in this system. Unless a motor carrier in the SMS has received an UNSATISFACTORY safety rating pursuant to 49 CFR Part 385, or has otherwise been ordered to discontinue operations by the FMCSA, it is authorized to operate on the nation's roadways.
Motor carrier safety ratings are available at http://safer.fmcsa.dot.gov and motor carrier licensing and insurance status are available at [http://li-public.fmcsa.dot.gov/](http://li-public.fmcsa.dot.gov) .
Section Title:  > Learn About the CSA Prioritization Preview
Content:
FMCSA is proposing a new prioritization methodology to keep enforcement efforts focused on the carriers in most need of intervention. Learn more about these changes and how they will improve highway safety.
[Visit the CSA Prioritization Preview](https://csa.fmcsa.dot.gov/PrioritizationPreview)

Section Title:  > Carrier History
Content:
The Carrier Measurement History Report displays six monthly runs. The default is
the current month's results followed by the five previous. To change the monthly
run dates, use the previous and next buttons. You can also choose to view quarterly
or semi-annual reports.
The report displays the BASIC values that were available on the SMS website for

Section Title:  > Average PU × Utilization Factor (as of 1/30/2026, updated monthly)
Content:
The SMS normalizes the Unsafe Driving and Crash Indicator BASICs measure by carrier size (i.e., a hybrid power unit and vehicle miles travelled measure) to account for exposure. Carrier size is defined as average power units multiplied by the utilization factor.
10 Average PU × Utilization Factor = 10 Average Power Units × 1 Utilization Factor
Average Power Units
Utilization Factor
In order to account for changes in a carrier's fleet size during the previous 24 months an average power unit value is used in the calculation to determine a carrier's Unsafe Driving BASIC and Crash Indicator measures.
10 Average Power Units = 10 Current Power Units + 10 Power Units 6 Months Ago + 10 Power Units 18 Months Ago
3
The Utilization Factor is a multiplier that adjusted the average PU values based on the utilization in terms of VMT per average PU.

###### FAST Act of 2015:

Readers should not draw conclusions about a carrier's overall safety condition simply based on the data displayed in this system. Unless a motor carrier has received an UNSATISFACTORY safety rating under part 385 of title 49, Code of Federal Regulations, or has otherwise been ordered to discontinue operations by the Federal Motor Carrier Safety Administration, it is authorized to operate on the Nation's roadways.

###### Safety Measurement System:

The data in the Safety Measurement System (SMS) is performance data used by the Agency and Enforcement Community. Asymbol, based on that data, indicates that FMCSA may prioritize a motor carrier for further monitoring.

Thesymbol is not intended to imply any federal safety rating of the carrier pursuant to 49 USC 31144. Readers should not draw conclusions about a carrier's overall safety condition simply based on the data displayed in this system. Unless a motor carrier in the SMS has received an UNSATISFACTORY safety rating pursuant to 49 CFR Part 385, or has otherwise been ordered to discontinue operations by the FMCSA, it is authorized to operate on the nation's roadways.

Motor carrier safety ratings are available at <http://safer.fmcsa.dot.gov> and motor carrier licensing and insurance status are available at [http://li-public.fmcsa.dot.gov/](http://li-public.fmcsa.dot.gov) .

## Average PU × Utilization Factor (as of 1/30/2026, updated monthly)

The SMS normalizes the Unsafe Driving and Crash Indicator BASICs measure by carrier size (i.e., a hybrid power unit and vehicle miles travelled measure) to account for exposure. Carrier size is defined as average power units multiplied by the utilization factor.

10 Average PU × Utilization Factor = 10 Average Power Units × 1 Utilization Factor

* Average Power Units
* Utilization Factor

In order to account for changes in a carrier's fleet size during the previous 24 months an average power unit value is used in the calculation to determine a carrier's Unsafe Driving BASIC and Crash Indicator measures.

10 Average Power Units = 10 Current Power Units \+ 10 Power Units 6 Months Ago \+ 10 Power Units 18 Months Ago  
3

The Utilization Factor is a multiplier that adjusted the average PU values based on the utilization in terms of VMT per average PU.

1. Determine Carrier Segment: **Combo**  
   Combination trucks/motor coach buses constituting 70% or more of the total PU  
   U.S. DOT# 461949 = 100% Combination trucks/motor coach buses
2. Determine VMT per Average Power Unit
   
   50,535 VMT per Average Power Unit = 505,350 VMT From Investigation (MCS-151) ÷ 10 Average Power Units
3. Based on Step 1 and 2 calculate the Utilization Factor
   
   |Meets Criteria |VMT per Avgerage PU |Utilization Factor |
   | --- | --- | --- |
   | |< 80,000 |1 |
   | |80,000 – 160,000 |1 + (VMT per PU − 80,000) ÷ 133,333 |
   | |160,000 – 200,000 |1\.6 |
   | |> 200,000 |1 |
   | |No Recent VMT Information |1 |

##### Use of SMS Data/Information

###### FAST Act of 2015:

Readers should not draw conclusions about a carrier's overall safety condition simply based on the data displayed in this system. Unless a motor carrier has received an UNSATISFACTORY safety rating under part 385 of title 49, Code of Federal Regulations, or has otherwise been ordered to discontinue operations by the Federal Motor Carrier Safety Administration, it is authorized to operate on the Nation's roadways.

###### Safety Measurement System:

The data in the Safety Measurement System (SMS) is performance data used by the Agency and Enforcement Community. Asymbol, based on that data, indicates that FMCSA may prioritize a motor carrier for further monitoring.

Thesymbol is not intended to imply any federal safety rating of the carrier pursuant to 49 USC 31144. Readers should not draw conclusions about a carrier's overall safety condition simply based on the data displayed in this system. Unless a motor carrier in the SMS has received an UNSATISFACTORY safety rating pursuant to 49 CFR Part 385, or has otherwise been ordered to discontinue operations by the FMCSA, it is authorized to operate on the nation's roadways.

Motor carrier safety ratings are available at <http://safer.fmcsa.dot.gov> and motor carrier licensing and insurance status are available at [http://li-public.fmcsa.dot.gov/](http://li-public.fmcsa.dot.gov) .
